title: vinca minor subsp. media
intro: Vinca minor subsp. media, also known as periwinkle, is a subspecies of the common periwinkle plant, characterized by its dark green, glossy leaves and blue-purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Vinca minor subsp. media include its potential to treat conditions such as high blood pressure, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, are often used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
